ski_mountaineering.notes: Muir Snowfield, Emmons Glacier, Fuhrer Finger — premier PNW ski mountaineering with 9,000+ ft descents
South Climb / SW Chutes are among the best ski descents in WA; mellow angle, huge vertical
Fryingpan Glacier and east face offer big ski lines on WA's third highest peak; steep and committing
Easton Glacier, Coleman-Deming, Roman Wall — deep snowpack, long season, accessible for spring ski tours
Sitkum, Chocolate, Cool glaciers all ski well; long approach (20+ mi) limits popularity
Mary Green Glacier offers moderate ski terrain; long approach via Holden Village or Railroad Creek
Cascadian Couloir is an excellent spring ski line (Apr-Jun); Sherpa Glacier also skis well

larch_scenery.notes: Ingalls Lake approach has scattered larch groves; Ingalls Creek drainage has larch at higher elevations

ski_mountaineering.notes: South-side couloirs hold snow into early summer; approach via Phelps Creek adds mileage

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek approach through Spider Meadow larch; fall colors along the entire valley

ski_mountaineering.notes: Sulphide Glacier and White Salmon Glacier are premier spring ski tours; summit pyramid requires boot/crampon
North face has excellent steep skiing; approach via Cascade Pass / Boston Basin or Horseshoe Basin
Nohokomeen Glacier offers moderate ski terrain; very remote, requires SR 20 to be open
Douglas Glacier and Banded Glacier have skiable terrain; extremely remote, multi-day approach
NE couloir holds steep snow into summer; remote approach via Lake Chelan and Company Creek
Dry east-side scramble peak; snow melts early, terrain is mostly talus and heather
Moderate glacier terrain on approach; not a destination ski peak but can combo with Fernow/Maude circuit

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek drainage has larch on approach; often combined with Fernow/Maude larch circuit

ski_mountaineering.notes: Silver Glacier has skiable terrain; extremely remote multi-day trip via Depot Creek or Chilliwack River
South-face couloirs offer spring ski options; approach via Phelps Creek, often paired with Maude

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek / Spider Meadow larch corridor on approach; combines alpine climbing with peak fall color

ski_mountaineering.notes: Lewis Glacier and south-face snow slopes offer enjoyable skiing; requires SR 20 open for access

larch_scenery.notes: Blue Lake / Heather-Maple Pass larch are legendary; approach from Wing Lake or Rainy Pass through prime larch

ski_mountaineering.notes: Ice Cliff Glacier and south couloir ski well; approach via Phelps Creek (gate may add 3 mi)

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek / Spider Meadow approach passes prime larch groves; Ice Cliff Glacier framed by golden larch

ski_mountaineering.notes: Redoubt Glacier offers moderate terrain; extremely remote — typically combined with Spickard as a multi-day
Dome Glacier has excellent moderate-angle skiing; very long approach via Downey Creek or Bachelor Creek
Eldorado Glacier is an early-season classic — accessible from Cascade River Rd, moderate angle, great corn skiing
Technical rock summit; approach couloirs can be skied but the peak itself is a rock climb
Triple Couloirs and Colchuck Glacier are classic steep ski lines; spring corn conditions ideal

larch_scenery.notes: Colchuck Lake / Aasgard Pass larch groves visible on approach; classic fall photo destination

ski_mountaineering.notes: Challenger Glacier has moderate terrain; extremely remote Picket Range — multi-day commitment
NE Couloir is a popular steep ski line; excellent spring corn above Colchuck Lake

larch_scenery.notes: Aasgard Pass and lower Enchantments larch on approach; Colchuck Lake + golden larch backdrop

ski_mountaineering.notes: Classic ski tour line; Clark Glacier offers excellent skiing, usually approached from Phelps Creek or Entiat River

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek / Larch Lakes area; extensive larch groves on approach from multiple directions

ski_mountaineering.notes: Dry east-side scramble; mostly talus and heather slopes, snow melts early
Extremely remote Picket Range; technical rock and glacier, not a practical ski objective
Small glaciers and couloirs on north side hold snow; remote approach requires SR 20
Extremely avalanche-prone north face; ice caves area below is a known hazard zone, not a safe ski objective

name: Mount Olympus
nearest_snotel_note: No verified Olympics SNOTEL; using Paradise (5150') as rough proxy — interpret with caution for this west-side peak

ski_mountaineering.notes: Blue Glacier and Snow Dome ski well; long 18-mi approach via Hoh River trail, multi-day trip
Silver Star Glacier and Wine Spires couloirs are enjoyable ski lines; requires SR 20 for access

larch_scenery.notes: Washington Pass / Blue Lake area larch visible on approaches; Cedar Creek has scattered larch

ski_mountaineering.notes: Sahale Glacier and Who-Me Couloir are popular ski lines; accessible via Cascade Pass when road opens
Technical rock peak with heavily bushed approach; not a ski objective
Middle Cascade Glacier approach offers moderate skiing; remote — accessed from Cascade Pass or Downey Creek
Colonial/Neve Glacier system provides ski access; approach via Colonial Creek when Diablo Dam road open
Roosevelt and Coleman glaciers give good skiing on the Baker massif; combine with Baker summit trips
Dana Glacier approach has moderate ski terrain; very remote, usually combined with Dome Peak trips
Glaciated north side has some ski potential; remote access via Entiat River or Phelps Creek

larch_scenery.notes: Entiat River drainage; scattered larch at treeline elevations

ski_mountaineering.notes: South-face couloirs hold snow in spring; steep and committing, approach via Mountaineer Creek
Sherpa Glacier offers moderate ski terrain; combine with Stuart Range traverses

larch_scenery.notes: Enchantments / Stuart Range; larch visible from Sherpa Glacier approach in fall

ski_mountaineering.notes: North-side glacier/snow slopes hold snow; remote, accessed via Entiat River

larch_scenery.notes: Entiat drainage approach has larch at higher elevations

ski_mountaineering.notes: Primarily a rock scramble in the Enchantments; no sustained ski-worthy slopes on the peak itself

larch_scenery.notes: Upper Enchantments — world-famous golden larch groves in Oct, one of the best larch displays in WA

ski_mountaineering.notes: Rocky scramble peak in the Enchantments; no significant skiable terrain

larch_scenery.notes: Upper Enchantments larch basins; peak-larch views from all angles in early-mid Oct

ski_mountaineering.notes: Classic ski tour line; north aspect holds quality snow, remote approach via Phelps Creek, often combined with 7FJ/Maude

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek approach passes through larch; often combined with 7FJ/Maude larch trips

ski_mountaineering.notes: Glacier cirque between Fortress and Chiwawa has moderate terrain; Phelps Creek approach

larch_scenery.notes: Spider Meadow / Phelps Creek approach through dense larch groves; golden valley views

ski_mountaineering.notes: Lyman Glacier area has moderate skiing; long approach via Phelps Creek or Chiwawa River

larch_scenery.notes: Larch Lakes and Spider Meadow approaches pass through extensive larch groves

ski_mountaineering.notes: Snow slopes on north and east aspects; typically combined with other Entiat peaks

larch_scenery.notes: Entiat River / Phelps Creek approaches pass through larch groves at treeline

ski_mountaineering.notes: East-side scramble; mostly heather, talus, and dry ridge walking
North-face couloirs hold snow into early summer; east-side approach from Entiat River valley

larch_scenery.notes: Lake Chelan-Sawtooth area has scattered larch; fall color on approach from Entiat side

ski_mountaineering.notes: Klawatti Glacier is a well-known ski descent; pair with Austera for a glacier traverse
East-side scramble peak; mostly dry talus and heather, not a ski objective
Snow slopes on north aspects; typically combined with other Entiat/Chiwawa area peaks

larch_scenery.notes: Entiat River drainage has larch at treeline; combined with other Entiat peaks in fall

ski_mountaineering.notes: North-side snow holds into summer; approach via Phelps Creek or Chiwawa River

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek / Chiwawa River approach passes through larch zones at higher elevations

ski_mountaineering.notes: Glacier between Emerald and 7FJ has moderate terrain; usually combined with Maude/7FJ trips

larch_scenery.notes: Phelps Creek approach has larch; glacier cirque between Emerald and 7FJ with scattered larch below

ski_mountaineering.notes: North-side snow slopes; remote, accessed via Phelps Creek or Entiat River

larch_scenery.notes: Entiat / Phelps Creek approach with larch at higher elevations

ski_mountaineering.notes: Dry east-side scramble; mostly talus, snow melts early on this Pasayten peak
Short rock scramble near Rainier's Tatoosh Range; too small and rocky for ski mountaineering
Monitor Ridge and Worm Flows are PNW spring ski classics; permits required May 15 - Oct 31, smooth volcanic terrain
South side, Sandy Glacier Headwall, Cooper Spur — year-round skiing potential, PNW classic
Sloan Glacier offers moderate skiing on the west side; Corkscrew route approach, Mtn Loop Hwy access limited in spring
Steep, brushy approach with technical rock; not a practical ski objective
Rocky summit with lookout; long trail approach, no sustained ski terrain
Avalanche Gulch, Hotlum-Bolam, Casaval Ridge — massive volcanic ski descents, 7,000+ ft runs
Technical rock climbing peak; brushy approach, no skiable terrain
Steep couloirs on south face hold snow; approached from Cascade Pass area, committing terrain
McAllister Glacier approach; often paired with Klawatti for a glacier ski traverse
Remote bushwhack peak; no significant ski terrain
Technical rock peak; remote, accessed via Hozomeen Lake, not a ski objective
Colonial Glacier is a pleasant ski run; accessible from Colonial Creek campground when road open
North side glacial terrain; remote approach, often climbed as part of Ptarmigan Traverse
North-side glaciers hold snow; extremely remote, multi-day trip — often combined with Spickard/Redoubt
Adjacent to Custer; glacial terrain on approach, same remote multi-day access
Buck Creek Glacier area offers moderate terrain; long approach via Buck Creek trail
Kimtah and surrounding glaciers offer moderate terrain; accessed via SR 20 corridor
Technical rock climbing peak; no practical ski terrain on the peak itself
North-side neve and glacier terrain; accessed from Neve Glacier/Colonial area
Dry east-side peak; scramble terrain, snow melts early
Remote ridge traverse; technical terrain, not suited for skiing
Dry east-side scramble; mostly talus and heather, similar to N Gardner
Technical rock spire; no skiable terrain
Glaciated approach on Ptarmigan Traverse; south-side snowfields offer moderate skiing
North-side Jerry Glacier has moderate terrain; remote access requires SR 20
North-face couloirs hold snow; accessed from Harts Pass area when road opens mid-summer

ski_mountaineering.notes: Horseshoe Glacier area has moderate terrain; approach via Phelps Creek or Chiwawa River

Remote technical rock spires; extremely committing approach, not a ski objective
Remote Picket Range; technical rock climbing required, not suited for skiing
Katsuk Glacier has moderate terrain; remote access via Fisher Creek when SR 20 is open
Dry Pasayten Wilderness peak; gentle heather slopes, snow melts very early
North-facing snow slopes; approach via Entiat River, often combined with nearby Entiat peaks
